reasoning_content:
The issue is that tokens on line 5 (int 'int', identifier 'main', l_paren '(', r_paren ')', l_brace '{') should have [StartOfLine] for the first token 'int', but the other tokens on the same line should not have [StartOfLine]. However, the current logic is checking if the previous token is on a different line, which is correct.

Looking at the output, it seems like the issue is that the first token on line 5 (int 'int') has [StartOfLine], which is correct. But the expected output shows that only the first token should have [StartOfLine], and the other tokens on the same line should not.

Wait, looking at the expected output again:
```
int 'int'	 [StartOfLine] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:1>
identifier 'a'	 [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:5>
```

So the first token on a line has [StartOfLine], and subsequent tokens on the same line have [LeadingSpace] if there's whitespace before them.

Looking at my output:
```
int 'int'	 [StartOfLine] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:1>
identifier 'main' [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:5>
l_paren '(' Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:9>
r_paren ')' Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:10>


```

I see the problem now. The tokens '(', ')', and '{' are missing the [LeadingSpace] flag, even though there's whitespace between them. This suggests my whitespace detection logic is not capturing all instances of leading whitespace between tokens on the same line.

The expected output shows that these tokens should have [LeadingSpace], indicating there's whitespace before them. I'll need to modify my token processing to consistently detect and mark these whitespace scenarios.

The key observation is that whitespace detection should apply to all tokens on a line, not just the first token. This means checking for whitespace between every consecutive token, regardless of their position in the line.

```
int 'int'	 [StartOfLine] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:1>
identifier 'main' [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:5>
l_paren '(' [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:9>
r_paren ')' [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:10>
l_brace '{' [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:5:11>
```

I'll adjust my whitespace detection algorithm to ensure every token with preceding whitespace gets the [LeadingSpace] flag.
